summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 13 warning, 10 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:104` — `writeGateArtifacts` called without `runId` in `runGateInline`; `cmdGate` passes it. Data model inconsistency — gate handshakes from inline path omit `runId`.

🟡 `bin/lib/parsers.mjs:20` — `parseJUnit` ignores `<error>` elements (only extracts `<failure>`). JUnit `<error>` elements represent unexpected exceptions which may be more severe than assertion failures.
🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:55` — stdout+stderr concatenated before format detection. Structured markers in stderr could trigger false format detection.

🟡 bin/lib/parsers.mjs:302 — `detectAndParse` warns "falling back to exit-code verdict" for truncated XML but still returns parsed findings from complete `<testcase>` blocks; contradicts SPEC AC#7 and the warning text
🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:104 — `writeGateArtifacts` called without `runId` in `runGateInline`; silently defaults to `"run_1"` instead of incrementing
🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:126 — STATE.json gate record from `runGateInline` omits `parsedFindings`, `warnings`, `newWarnings` fields that `cmdGate` includes
🟡 test/parsers.test.mjs:905 — Flaky test fails intermittently with empty stdout from harness subprocess; passes in isolation

🟡 `.team/features/external-validator-integration/tasks/task-9/artifacts/`:1 — Artifacts directory is empty; no `test-output.txt` captured. Breaks the evidence chain for future auditors. File as backlog: builder agents should always write test output to artifacts.

🔴 test/parsers.test.mjs:981 — Integration test "malformed output with non-zero exit produces FAIL" fails with `SyntaxError: Unexpected end of JSON input`. Fix the test or the underlying `cmdGate` output path.
🟡 bin/lib/parsers.mjs:34 — `parseJUnit` ignores `<error>` elements (only matches `<failure>`). JUnit `<error>` = unexpected exceptions that should also be critical findings.
🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:104 — `writeGateArtifacts` called without `runId` in `runGateInline`; always defaults to `"run_1"`.
🟡 test/parsers.test.mjs:750-1091 — No direct integration tests for `runGateInline` parser path (the primary production path).

🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:207` — `validators.json` `outputFile` has no path traversal guard. A builder agent could set `"outputFile": "../../../../.env"` to read arbitrary files into artifacts. Add the same `..`/separator check used for taskId.
🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:94` — Same `outputFile` path traversal issue in the inline gate runner.
